description_long: This object is a pair of World War II-era German tanks, specifically modeled after the Panzerkampfwagen V Panther. Each tank features a long-barreled main cannon mounted on a rotating turret, with prominent hatches and ventilator domes on the turret roof. The hulls are angular and heavily armored, with visible side skirts and mesh screens for additional protection. The tanks are equipped with wide, robust tracks and detailed running gear, suitable for traversing rough urban terrain. The exterior is finished in a uniform gray paint, with subtle weathering and metallic highlights, but no visible camouflage or military insignia. The tanks' design emphasizes both firepower and protection, with reinforced armor plating covering the entire body.
description: A pair of gray, heavily armored WWII-era German tanks with long cannons, angular hulls, and detailed tracks, lacking visible camouflage or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the angular glacis plates, main cannons, and frontal armor details of both tanks.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the main guns, side skirts, mesh screens, and the full track assemblies.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back armor, exhausts, and rear hull details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the long barrels, side armor, and track mechanisms.
attribute_weapon_mounts: Two main cannon mounts, one per turret, centrally located on each tank's turret front.
attribute_armor_plating: Full body armor plating with sloped and reinforced panels covering the hull, turret, and side skirts; thickness appears substantial and consistent throughout.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; solid gray paint scheme.
